idea: 1p2c split-output WN=128 STAGES=2 non-persistent fp16->fp32; first functional baseline at 198.6 TFLOPS
expected_gain: establish baseline, expect 300+ TFLOPS with optimization

idea: Switched from 1p2c to 1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3: reduced CTA threads from 384 to 256, enabling 2+ CTAs/SM vs prior 1 CTA/SM at 128 regs/thread
Persistent 1p1c WN=256 STAGES=4 with 114 CTAs: only 114 parallel CTAs serializes 2352 tiles each per CTA, causing massive execution serialization vs non-persistent 16384 CTAs
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3 maxrregcount=160: same as iter002 at 291 TFLOPS, register spilling has no benefit
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3 padded output SMEM: same result 292T, padding did not reduce bank conflicts since Choreo already handles layout. Root issue is SMEM at 184KB prevents 2 CTAs/SM
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 hoped for 2 CTAs/SM via SMEM reduction: got worse at 206T vs 291T at WN=256. Halving WN halves arithmetic intensity per CTA, losing more than we gain from extra CTAs
1p1c WN=192 STAGES=2: 196T, even worse than WN=256. Narrower tile consistently loses. Must stay at WN=256 and fix latency differently
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=4: increased stages from 3 to 4, slight improvement 291T->293T. More prefetch depth slightly reduces CTA barrier wait. SMEM=224KB barely fits in 228KB
